response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong software development skills and a diverse technical background, but his experience is largely in full-stack, web, and mobile development rather than the specialized hardware, SoC, and AI accelerator performance optimization required for this role. While he has worked with low-latency systems and some embedded projects, his profile lacks direct evidence of the deep hardware-software co-design expertise or AI workload optimization experience sought for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: Extensive experience with low-latency systems and performance-critical applications (e.g., micro-services at Sociomantic, real-time note-taking tools).
Diverse technical background including embedded systems, open-source contributions, and tooling development (e.g., GIS platforms, test frameworks).
Strong problem-solving and leadership skills, evidenced by workshops, talks, and founding an open-source Geo-Data platform.
concerns: Lacks direct experience with SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, or hardware-software co-design for deep-learning workloads.
No clear evidence of SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU workload analysis, or system-on-chip design expertise in resume.
Most recent roles focus on full-stack/web development and AI-agent infrastructure, not hardware optimization or cloud infrastructure for AI accelerators.
